rationale: The prior block failed because each `post*_self_@[p as int] =~= pre_self_@[p as int]` assertion lives inside the proof body, where the ensures-antecedent of `det_<f>` is not yet a hypothesis (only at function exit). Re-wrap the entire derivation in `if (ensures_conjunction) { ... }` so every ensures-conjunct becomes an in-scope hypothesis; then close the disjoint-trigger gap on the two `forall|p: IOid|`s by chaining each through `pre_self_@[p as int]`, and pin every per-projection equality at the `ioid` slot via the same `pre_self_@[ioid as int].unwrap()` pivot. Verus's path-sensitive reasoning lifts these into `ensures_conj ==> assert` outside the `if`, which discharges `det_<f>_equal` at exit.
